warnings.message: seasonId 'season-1' does not literally match the world's seasons array entry 'Season 1: First Year' (a display string, not a slug). Likely intentional (id vs. display name convention) but worth confirming this mapping is documented somewhere.
warnings.suggestion: Add a seasons metadata table on the world object mapping ids like 'season-1' to display names, or rename world.seasons to store {id, name} objects for unambiguous cross-referencing.

warnings.message: scene-3a's transition.nextScenes array lists 'scene-4' twice — harmless since both of choice-2a's options route to the same scene, but redundant.
warnings.suggestion: Could collapse to a single 'scene-4' entry for clarity, or leave as-is since it doesn't break routing.

warnings.message: scene-3b's transition.nextScenes lists 'scene-4' three times for choice-2b's three options, all converging on the same scene — same redundancy pattern as scene-3a.
warnings.suggestion: No functional issue; consider a comment/convention note that repeated entries indicate convergent branches.

warnings.message: Jordan is present in scene-9's characters list and has story relevance to the climax, but has no dialogue line reacting to the pivotal 'whose idea was this' question — only Priya reacts (s9-6). Given Jordan's established conflict-avoidant character trait, a beat acknowledging his presence could strengthen the scene, though this is not required.
warnings.suggestion: Optionally add a Jordan reaction line for consistency with his established discomfort-avoidance behavior pattern from earlier scenes.

recommendations: Consider standardizing season identifiers between the World record and Episode record (e.g., both using 'season-1' as an id with a separate human-readable name field) to prevent future cross-referencing ambiguity as more episodes are added.
The branching structure is clean and all paths (scene-1 through the three ending variants to END) are reachable and terminate correctly — no dead ends or orphaned scenes detected.
Trait mapping coverage is complete and proportional across all five choice points; INTEGRITY, COMMUNICATION, and JUDGMENT are all exercised multiple times as intended by targetTraits.
Character pronoun consistency (Alex: they/them, Priya: she/her, Jordan: he/him, Sol: she/her) held up throughout all dialogue and narrator/internal lines — no slippage found.
This episode is ready to move from DRAFT to IN_REVIEW status given the absence of blocking or critical issues.
